changes.description: Export: Privacy blur zones no longer crash Advanced Editor exports with 'code 4294967274'. The blur mask was sized to the quality preset while AE camera tiles render at their own per-tile size; the mismatch killed the encode before a single frame was written. Masks now always match the tile they blur.

Export: Fixed 'Minimap render timeout' aborting the Live Map (Slow) pre-render on some machines. Chromium throttles animation callbacks in hidden windows, which stalled the offscreen map renderer; background throttling is now disabled for that window.

versions.title: Liquid Glass Redesign, Export Reliability & Performance

changes.description: Theme: Complete Liquid Glass redesign matching the SentryCloud / Sentry Drive look — translucent frosted panels with specular edges, an ambient green glow behind the UI, the SentryCloud green accent replacing cyan throughout, and new Inter + JetBrains Mono typography. The light theme has been retired; the app is now dark-only.

Theme: The Glass Blur slider in Settings now drives every glass surface live — the sidebar, dashboard and map widgets, all modals (Settings, Export, Advanced Editor, changelog, and more), toasts, and the export progress pill — with its range extended to 30px for a fully frosted look.

Drives: Drive cards redesigned to match Sentry Drive — departure and arrival times joined by a route track with origin/destination pins, real street and place names for where each drive started and ended (resolved via OpenStreetMap, cached on disk, fetched only for cards on screen), a red disengagement line, and pill chips for distance, duration, FSD percentage, and available footage.
Sidebar: The classic sidebar now wears Sentry Drive's ambient panel backdrop, and both sidebars use its glass card styling for clips and drives, with sticky date headers that footage scrolls beneath.

Drives: Loading a large drive-data.json no longer freezes the app when parsing finishes. The entire GPS dataset previously crossed between processes in one enormous message; the drive list now receives lightweight stats and each drive's full route loads on demand when you open it.
Drives: The window stays smooth and draggable while drive data loads — parsing and grouping now run on worker threads instead of the app's main process, and the result is handed over zero-copy.

Sidebar: Resizing the classic sidebar is no longer laggy with large clip or drive lists. Width updates are batched to one per frame and off-screen cards skip layout entirely, which also makes long lists scroll and render faster.

Drives: Drive-data.json files load roughly 35% faster (the drive-tags pass runs in parallel on another CPU core), and re-opening the same unchanged file — including after a window reload — is instant instead of re-parsing for minutes.

Export: Fixed exports failing with 'code 4294967274' on unusual custom layouts. Camera tiles spread far apart could demand an output video larger than any encoder accepts; the export now uniformly scales the whole composition down to fit (max 8192px per side) instead of failing, and invalid layout data falls back to the standard grid.

Export: Output dimensions are now always padded to even values on the CPU encoder path too (previously GPU-only got 16-pixel alignment), eliminating a class of 'Invalid argument' encoder startup failures.
Export: Selecting a zero-length range (start and end markers at the same spot) now shows a clear warning instead of failing cryptically or writing an empty file.
Export: Error messages no longer blame the GPU encoder when the failure happened elsewhere, and failure logs now include the canvas size and active encoder to make support reports easier to diagnose.

Export: Custom multi-camera layouts now composite in a single pass when tiles don't overlap. Measured on a 4-camera High export: GPU exports went from 2.1x to 3.5x realtime, CPU exports from 1.2x to 2.2x realtime.

Export: CPU encoding now picks its thread count adaptively from CPU cores, available RAM, and canvas size, replacing the old fixed 4-thread cap. Many-core machines encode dramatically faster; low-RAM machines are still protected from swapping.
Export: The Live Map (Slow) minimap now pre-renders at 12fps instead of 36fps - about 3x faster for that stage with no visible difference at minimap size.

Playback: Dashboard, G-force meter, compass, map, and steering wheel overlays now skip redundant screen updates when values haven't visibly changed, cutting CPU usage during playback - especially on parked Sentry footage. Overlays you've hidden no longer do any work at all.

Playback: Telemetry from recently viewed clips is cached (capped at 8 clips, a few MB of RAM), so jumping back to a recent segment shows speed and GPS data instantly instead of re-reading the whole video file.
Clips: Event metadata files now load in parallel and no longer block the app process, so folders with many Sentry events populate their badges noticeably faster.

Editor: Added the Advanced Editor — a canvas-based multi-camera layout where you can freely position and resize each camera tile and overlay (timestamp, dashboard, minimap), with native aspect-locked resize, snap-to-edge guides, and per-element scale controls. Exports honor the layout exactly, with the export canvas aspect ratio driven by what you arrange.

Export: Added the Tesla Screen Dash dashboard overlay style — a top-right HUD that mimics the in-car driving display, with PRND, large speed readout, MPH/KPH unit, Self-Driving / Manual label, a vertical regen/accel bar, and a wall-clock readout. Auto-configures a small dark minimap in the top-right corner on first use.
Playback: Added bindable keyboard shortcuts for playback speed — Speed Up, Speed Down, Reset to 1x, and Max (4x). Assign keys in Settings → Keyboard Shortcuts; no defaults set.

SentryUSB: Drive-data.json files larger than 512 MB now load correctly. The renderer previously hit V8's max string length when reading 1 GB+ files; parsing now streams in the main process and only the small grouped-drives result crosses IPC.

Export: Advanced Editor exports now work reliably across all encoders (NVENC, AMF, QuickSync, VAAPI, VideoToolbox, and libx264). Output is always padded to a 16-pixel multiple (was previously GPU-only), and overlarge AE layouts auto-downscale to fit the encoder ceiling with a user-visible toast instead of hard-failing.

Startup: Removed the 'Checking for updates…' overlay on launch. FFmpeg detection now runs asynchronously (no main-thread freeze) and the update check runs silently in the background — the app is interactive immediately on launch.
Drives tab: Now shows a loading spinner with progress while a large drive-data.json is being streamed and grouped, instead of looking like 'No drive data loaded' the whole time.

SentryUSB: Picking the same drive-data.json multiple times (e.g., re-selecting in Settings while the startup auto-load is still in flight) no longer kicks off parallel parses — duplicate loads of the same file reuse the in-flight promise.
Linux: Added hardware-accelerated export via VAAPI for AMD and Intel integrated GPUs, on par with the existing NVENC path on Windows.

App size: Swapped the native image library used for privacy blur masks for a pure-JS equivalent. Removes roughly 100 MB of platform-specific binaries from every installer on every platform, with no change to blur output.
Export: The Detailed dashboard now shows the clip's recording date and time as its own row at the top, using your existing date and time format preferences.
Startup: Added a 'Checking for updates…' loader so the first few seconds of FFmpeg detection and the update check no longer look like a freeze.

Export: Maximum quality now works on Intel integrated GPUs. HEVC QuickSync previously failed to open the encoder at resolutions like 4344x2088; the canvas is now padded to a 16-pixel multiple for GPU encoders and the app automatically falls back to the CPU encoder if the GPU encoder still can't start.
Export: Encoder-initialization failures no longer show the misleading "Invalid file path, try a shorter path" error.
macOS: Auto-updates now use smaller delta downloads via electron-updater, with automatic install on restart.
UI: Migrated most icons to Google Material Symbols for a cleaner, more consistent look.
Export: Steering wheel in the detailed dashboard now correctly reflects Autopilot state.
Map: Event pins now snap to the GPS polyline instead of drifting off-route.
Zoom: Fixed video-container overflow breaking when a clip is zoomed.
UI: Privacy zone restore banner now updates when the language is changed.
UI: Restored the DOM guard on the thumbnail tooltip to prevent intermittent crashes.
Developer: Added a manual pre-release install option in the Developer modal for testing upcoming builds.
Font: Switched to Noto Sans and Noto Sans Mono via Google Fonts
Export: Privacy blur restore banner is now fully translated in all supported languages.
Compact dashboard: Reorganized layout regarding speed, gear, and autopilot state.
Compact dashboard: Gear state text is now always white and bold for better readability.
Compact dashboard: Fixed accelerator pedal fill not covering the full pedal shape.
Export: Added a confirmation dialog when canceling an in-progress export to prevent accidental cancellation.
Export: Partial export files are now automatically deleted when an export is canceled.
Footage and export folders are now remembered separately changing your input folder no longer affects where the export dialog opens, and vice versa.
Export filenames now use the clip's recording date and time instead of today's date. The filename reflects when the footage was actually recorded, including the hour and minute.
Export: Improve mobile quality without inflating file size
Export: Fix timelapse compression artifacts
Export: *MAJOR* Fix blur zones failing silently on Windows installs
Export: Fix front camera compression in multi-camera grids
Map: Fix OSM tile blocking
UI : Fix compact dashboard speed/turn signal overlap
Speed unit now appears below the speed number on the Compact dashboard.
Fixed Bug With Privacy Zones Not showing up on HW3 Exports for users.
Fixed GPS track alignment for Static Maps. Track and arrow are now properly aligned with road.
Adjusted appearance and smoothness of route and arrow for Static Maps.
Enlarged autosteer icon for the Tesla Mobile export UI to match accelerator and brake icons.
Made minimap export settings persistent.
Fixed FSD percentage, disengagement, and accelerator push calculations to match Sentry USB accuracy.
Fixed event camera appearing as a secondary view when focusing a different camera tile.
Fixed preview thumbnails getting stuck on screen after hovering events.
Tesla Mobile dashboard polish: removed double steering wheel ring, bigger gear indicator, gray accel pedal fill.
Minimap now uses heading-up mode during drive playback with a compass toggle to switch between heading-up and north-up.
Share link now appears immediately while your clip is still uploading.
Choose how long your shared clip stays available — from 30 minutes to 7 days.
Mouse back/forward/middle buttons can now be bound as keyboard shortcuts.
Clip sharing time limit increased from 5 minutes to 10 minutes.
Pre-selected share now shows a clip preview before uploading — confirm with 'Confirm & Upload' instead of auto-uploading.
Fixed My Shared Clips modal cutting off content when selecting a clip.
NEW "Tesla Mobile" dashboard style that mimics Tesla's mobile app dashcam viewer UI.
friendly FFmpeg error messages & Fix HW3 front camera ratio distortion
export preview, share quality fix, UI polish
Add clip nav keybinds
Added thumbnails when hovering on Sentry & Saved Clips.

versions.title: Added Sentry USB Drive Tracking Support

changes.description: Added for Sentry USB drives tracking and viewing the footage. Including things like the route map for the entire drive.
We have added IPv6 support to Sentry Studio for the Clip Sharing, Updates, and other features such as the support chat.
Added a new more detailed dashboard layout to exports.
Improved translations throughout Sentry Studio.
Fixed the new tag not going away after interaction.

versions.title: Sentry Six is now Sentry Studio

changes.description: Our app Sentry Six has now been rebranded to Sentry Studio as we prepare to launch Sentry USB and Sentry Connect in the coming weeks.
The MacOS App is now signed and notarized by Apple for builds 2026.8.4 and above.
Increased SEI dashboard telemetry polling rate by 15x during playback. Speed, steering wheel, and all other telemetry values now update every frame instead of skipping values.
Steering wheel now tracks near 1:1 like Tesla's instrument cluster. Reduced peak lag from 170° to under 8° during fast maneuvers like U-turns and 3-point turns.
Added accelerator pedal position to the SEI Dashboard on exported videos.
Added an option to restore privacy zones that were used in the previous export.
Optimized Settings and Support Chat layout.
Added an light mode option for application wide in Settings. Along with a dark mode option for the minimap overlay.
Fixed an issue where non-Tesla dashcam folder structures would not load.
Fixed an issue where FFMPEG would freeze the entire application when you would open the export modal.
Fixed turn signal blink timing to match Tesla's 400ms on / 300ms off cycle for both the dashboard overlay and exported videos.
Added Time-lapse. Works with all current features and has been translated to all 13 languages. Added time-lapse exported time display (1:01:13 → 07:39)
Fixed bug with timeline where it would load things twice, causing desync of the cameras.
Export progress bars now move smoothly instead of jumping each percentage. Main progress bar will now also show 1% at a time instead of jumping. Overall smoothing of the progress bars.
Made blinker icons consistent with export icons in the UI.
Fixed a bug where changing the date format in settings caused all other dates to disappear, requiring reselection of the Tesla folder.
You can now generate a shareable link, that you can use to share your exports with others!
Fixed some bugs with the export modal, and dashboard overlay on exported videos.
Fixed translations not working in certain cases.
Cleaned up unused code and files.
Added Search Functionality to Settings.
Fixed an issue where users had to wait for the install button to appear because the download progress bar would not display on the update prompt. Despite it downloading.
Added the missing Mirror Cameras toggle in Settings back.
Overhauled the UI design to be more modern and user-friendly.
Increased the polling interval for support chat.
Added the ability to remove a folder/event using the Trash Can icon in the Clip Browser.
Fixed a critical issue that caused the Auto Updater to break on versions 2026.6.5 and 2026.6.6.
Fixed the Compact Dashboard Layout not resizing properly on smaller displays.
Fixed the Camera Glow/Highlight on Saved and Sentry Clip events not displaying properly.
Removed the fake brake autohold feature on the Dashboard to better represent what official is being shown by the SEI Data.
Added the accelerator pedal position to the SEI Dashboard on exported videos.
Added an Alpha Tag to the GPS Minimap export feature as it is still in development.
Added Fast Mini map improvement. This is only a fixed map not live like the main map for exporting.
improved live map export by 33% Thanks to Jeff
Update API To handle Updates. Client will now send Platform, Version, and UUID to help us with updates.
Added 12 hrs and 24 hrs settings to change format for Exported and UI Dashboards.
Added Map UI option to export clips (Beta)
Added option to  Load clips from a folder. No Tesla USB structure needed.
Added option to disable Mirrored cameras (L/R Repeaters, and Back camera) so they show Raw Orientation.
Removed Optimized Blur Due to Issue with it not working well with High contrast things. (Red text License plates)
Added New Blur Zone Options. Solid (Fast), Blur (Optimized But has issues with Red text/plates can be seen), True Blur (Slow).
Added Option for Solid Blur to Work with SEI Dashboard. (Blur, True Blur DO NOT WORK with Dashboard.)
Program Will now Auto select Language Based Screen Language OS is using.
Added more translation to other areas like (Support Chat, Settings tabs, Export module)
Fixed Some issues with Welcome module Not being translated and issues with HTML
Added Tool Tips Translation in all 13 Languages.
Fixed A bug where Translation was braking dynamic info of the Folders in clip browser.
Added Translations to Notifications for errors,info,etc
Fixed Steering Wheel being slightly Off in the UI for ALL Dashboards. Update Icon to 512x512 (Thanks JeffFromTheIRS)

Added Option for Accel Pedal to show Pressure.
- Added 3 options (On/Off (default), Fill (uses Icon As Bar), and Bar on Side) - Currently this is a UI Update only.

Added 13 NEW Languages:
Spanish,
French,
German,
Chinese (Simplified),
Japanese,
Korean,
Portuguese,
Russian,
Italian,
 Dutch,
Polish,
Turkish. (UI UPDATE ONLY NO EXPORT)
Added the ability for the map route line to display Blue/Gray depending on Self Driving/Manual driving state.
Added the ability to zoom in/out and pan around the map. (Hold and drag using right click to pan around the map)
Switched Map Data to OSM to resolve issues where CartoDB would not work in China.
Adjusted the consistency of the date/time formatting throughout the application. You can set your preferred format under Settings.
Fixed UTC time. We Changed time to UTC No matter the place. instead of Cars Timezone.
Fixed issue with MacOS exporting Compact Dashboard with Big assets.
Fixed an issue where the steering wheel would turn the wrong direction on exported video.
Fixed a bug with the time showing wrong by 1 hour during export.
Fixed an issue with MacOS exporting SEI Dashboard not looking similar to Windows.
Swapped positions on export overlay for time & gear. To give room to add the date when exporting.
Added an X-Large (55%) Option for Dashboard Size. This is Due to MacOS Retina making the dashboard look smaller then Windows. We recommend large (45%) for MacOS users.
Added date to SEI Dashboard when exporting.

versions.title: Fix Unicode characters & Update Dashboard Exporting

changes.description: Fixed an issue where Unicode characters were not being properly encoded causing folder/clip selection not working.
Improved the dashboard export layout and reduced the amount of time the pre-render takes by almost 99%.
Fixed an issue where Unicode characters were not being properly encoded causing folder/clip selection not working.
Bundled FFMPEG on MacOS instead of requiring homebrew installation.
Fixed an issue where Intel QuickSync would cause an export to fail.
Added a new Compact Dashboard option to Export. You can now choose between default and Compact.
Check out our new website at https://sentry-six.com
Fixed Compact Dashboard UI being able to be resized to small sized. Added a minimum size.
Added timestamp to Compact Dashboard UI.
Improved the support chat system. (You must update for the Support Chat system to work if you're on a old version.)
Fixed an issue where shortcuts would not register if you clicked the timeline.
Improved overall UI consistency and layout.
Fixed an issue where some shortcut keys would not work.
Fixed an issue where some buttons would stay focused and pressing the space key would trigger them.
Added the option to change the fast forward and backward duration under shortcuts.

versions.title: Addition of Export Blur Zones & Improvements

changes.description: You can now add blur zones to each individual camera before export. Blur zones can be useful to blur sensitive information before sharing such as, license plates, faces, locations, etc. Note that blur zones apply for the entire duration of the exported video.
Adjusted the TeslaCam folder selection to work with other folder names.

Added a new Support Ticket system which can be found with the support icon located on the playback bar. This replaces the old feedback and support id system. Leave feedback, bug reports and request troubleshooting through the support ticket system.
Adjusted the export progress bars to be sticky.
Fixed FFMPEG not being detected on Windows Automated Install
Fixed an issue where the Windows Installer failed to update due to an Application Still Open error.

versions.title: Added timestamp option for video exports

changes.description: Added an option to add a timestamp to exported footage without dashboard overlay burn in. There are additional date formats for international users.

Fixed an issue where the MacOS auto updater would not work on Automatic Installs. Affected Version: 2026.1.22. If you are on the affected version, please download the latest version from the GitHub releases page.
Added a new Send Feedback button that can be found in the Settings Menu. You can attach media to assist with feedback/bug reports.
Added a new compact dashboard layout which can be changed in the Settings menu.
Added MacOS Automatic Install .dmg file to current and all future releases.
Fixed an issue where the dashboard & map might disappear if you resized the application window after adjusting their position.
Fixed current time on export dashboard overlay being delayed. now will be accurate down to the Sec.
Fixed current time on export dashboard overlay not appearing.
Improved diagnostic system.
Fixed AMD GPU not being detected properly. Which was causing issues with GPU encoding.
Fixed UI issues and auto updater issues.
Fixed auto update system in preparation for .exe releases
Fixed AMD GPU not being detected properly.
Fixed map event pins not clearing properly.
Fixed video auto playing when paused and using seeking functions.
Fixed playback bar not updating after seeking while paused.
Revamped Diagnostic Support System to not rely on Third Party Services and added additional measures to protect user diagnostic data.
Reduced PII & not helpful support diagnostic data.
Updated default branch to main.
Added warning if no GPU is detected for export telemetry overlay toggle.
Cleaned up dead code & comments.
Removed legacy drag & drop code.
Removed some keybinds in favor of customizable keyboard shortcuts.
Added ability to check for updates without having to relaunch app.
Added ability to swap between Main and Dev-SEI Branch. Dev-SEI is now used for Beta.
Changed version naming to match year and week similar to Tesla's version naming.
Changed the camera export layout to match HW3/HW4 camera count.
Fixed playback bar jumping around to a different spot than selected.
Added new user welcome guide

versions.title: Add Classic Layout Setting & Ability to Minimize Export Modal during export

changes.description: Added ability to switch between Classic and Modern layout styles
Added ability to minimize export modal during export and see progress on a floating notification
Fixed a rare issue where updates would sometimes not work properly
Added a new diagnostics button to the settings modal that will generate a shareable id to share with the Sentry Studio team for debugging purposes.

versions.title: Prevents modal from closing during update

changes.description: Prevents the update modal from closing during an update if the user attempts to close it.
Fixed changelogs not loading properly. Updated to grab changelog from github repo instead of locally due to inital testing..
Improved video export marker UX and layout
Fixed rare issue of export cancellation not working properly
Fixed FFmpeg memory leak
Improved GPU encoding & added HEVC encoder detection
Significantly reduced export time when Dashboard Overlay is enabled
Added ability for Maximum Quality export to be done with GPU if HEVC is available
Overhaul of Settings & Export UI to improve tidiness and match the design language between the modals
Added changelog viewer in Settings
New version-based update system with semantic versioning
Inital Release
